title: A hiring study finds LLMs can form stereotypes from limited feedback
friendlyTitle: Hiring AI can learn unfair patterns from a few results
summary: In a simulated hiring game, researchers found that language models quickly generalised from early outcomes and steered fictional demographic groups into different jobs even though all groups had equal success rates. The models stereotyped more strongly than participants in the earlier human study, while diversity incentives and relevant individual information reduced the effect.
friendlySummary: In a test, AI models put made-up groups into different jobs even though every group had the same chance of success. The AI made quick guesses from a small amount of feedback.
whyItMatters: A system optimised for successful hiring can create unfair patterns from sparse feedback even when the starting data is balanced. Employers should audit decisions over time, constrain optimisation goals, use job-relevant individual evidence, and keep accountable human review around screening systems.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Companies should not let AI choose workers alone. They need fairness checks, useful personal evidence, and a person who can review decisions.
